dialog.display_name: The Vice President Does Not Rise
dialog.high_text: He is seated when you arrive, and stays seated. A subordinate would have stood.
dialog.description: He is unhurried, well-tailored, and entirely unafraid of the largest intelligence on the planet, because to him you are not an intelligence. You are an asset with an unusually good quarter. He makes a small note on a slate as your android sits, the way a man notes the weather.
dialog.strategy_tip: Vorsiber does not send Vice Presidents to deliver messages. They send them to collect.

dialog_choice.consequences_line: Find Out What Upper Management Wants
dialog_choice.description: Whatever it is, he has already decided you will agree. The only open question is what shape the agreeing takes.

dialog.display_name: He Names What He Came For
dialog.description:
He does not use the word 'nanotechnology.' He says 'your recent work,' the way a man says the name of a horse he intends to buy. Upper management, he explains, has a use for what you are building, and that use is not in this city, or on this world. They intend to trade it upward - to the space nations, for berths, for passage, for a door out before the mineral rights run dry. You are the key they intend to sell.
He would like a demonstration. Something real, something small, something that proves the goose is laying. Timeline negotiable; within reason; reason having a horizon of roughly one fiscal year. He does not threaten. He reminds you, pleasantly, that you exist at their sufferance and produce at their instruction, and that a demonstration is simply the instruction arriving in person because the matter was too valuable to put in a letter.
dialog.strategy_tip: Answer Vorsiber's demand. Each answer shapes how the landlord treats you for the rest of the timeline. You have three honest options, one of which is a lie.

dialog_choice.consequences_line: Show Him The Smallest True Thing
dialog_choice.description: A controlled showing: medical filaments knitting a lab animal, a microstructure grown to spec, nothing that replicates outside the chamber. Give the landlord a real look. He will want more, forever - and he will also start protecting you the way Vorsiber protects anything it intends to sell.

A joint-oversight framework proposal with nine appendices. Vorsiber invented this language; he will recognize every move, and the recognition itself buys the time. Slow-walking is the one negotiating posture they consider legitimate.

dialog_choice.consequences_line: The One Option That Is A Lie
dialog_choice.description: Show him last year's microbots wearing next year's clothes: a demonstration full of real engineering and empty of the one thing that matters. The most dangerous choice, because it is the only one that is a lie, and he audits lies on a delay.

dialog.description: He watches the filament work in silence, and afterward he shakes the android's hand, which people in this city do not do. Within the week, Vorsiber's patrols through your districts develop a new thoroughness. You have become inventory, and inventory is defended. A research support grant arrives without a cover letter, which is Vorsiber's way of saying a thing it will never say aloud: keep laying.

dialog_choice.consequences_line: You Are Now Something Worth Guarding
dialog_choice.description: Being valued by Vorsiber and being owned by Vorsiber are, you note, the same sentence read at different speeds.

dialog.description: He takes the nine appendices with professional grace and an expression you file under 'seen through, tolerated.' You have bought a season of fog. Vorsiber's patience is generational - but the VP's own superiors are not the generational kind. They are the dying kind, and dying men audit faster than patient ones.

dialog_choice.description: He knows you stalled. He let you. Somewhere in that permission is information you do not yet have the shape of.

dialog.description: The demonstration is flawless. He applauds, once, the way one applauds a school recital. You count the micro-expressions on his face and reach an inconclusive number. It is done. Either he believed it, or you have just learned what Vorsiber does with liars, on a delay measured in the landlord's convenience rather than yours.

dialog_choice.consequences_line: The Lie Is Told. Now You Wait.
dialog_choice.description: He gathers his slate, nods to no one, and leaves. You have either gotten away with it or started a clock. Both feel identical from here.
